title: Mechanical Engineer
how_to_become: Complete a Modern Apprenticeship or apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website.
description: NHS mechanical engineers install, repair, and maintain mechanical equipment to ensure it is in working condition and can be used safely. They work on heating, ventilation, air conditioning, hot water systems, dishwashers, sluice devices, and industrial laundry equipment.
role_overview: As a mechanical engineer, you'll maintain and repair heating and ventilation, steam distribution, air conditioning, and domestic water systems in hospitals and healthcare facilities.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education and an Engineering Modern Apprenticeship (4-year programme) are required.
qualifications: HNC Engineering Systems at SCQF level 7
HNC Mechanical Engineering at SCQF level 7

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

learning_and_development.overview: Work towards completing the Estates Workbook and complete specialised training in systems and equipment to comply with standards, laws, and national guidelines.

career_progression.overview: Gaining new qualifications leads to more senior roles with greater responsibilities in estates services.

chunks.text: To become a mechanical engineer in the NHS, complete a 4-year Engineering Modern Apprenticeship or apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website.

NHS mechanical engineers install, repair, and maintain mechanical equipment including heating, ventilation, air conditioning, hot water systems, dishwashers, sluice devices, and industrial laundry equipment.

Useful school subjects include English, Maths, Science, Physics, and Engineering Science. A 4-year Engineering Modern Apprenticeship is required. HNC Engineering Systems or HNC Mechanical Engineering at SCQF level 7 are also useful.
Gaining qualifications leads to more senior roles with greater responsibilities in estates services.
